category_title: Students with SEBD who respond to peer provocation with reactive aggression and 'payback' behaviors
scenario_title: Attempting to report peer bullying to a teacher
minimal_context: The student is meeting one-on-one with a teacher in the office to report a peer conflict that just occurred.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You asked to speak with me about what happened with the other students.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I get angry when people are annoying me. They were calling me names and hurting me, because they know they can get to me.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, well, we will touch on this later.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: If someone hurt me, like kicked me or something, you wouldn't do anything about it. I would overreact and start going off to them and trying to kick them.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You need to calm down right now.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I know that the teachers think that I'm bad, so they'll just go for their side. I don't bother to say anything.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: You asked to speak with me about what happened with the other students.

STUDENT: I get angry when people are annoying me. They were calling me names and hurting me, because they know they can get to me.

TUTOR: Okay, well, we will touch on this later.

STUDENT: If someone hurt me, like kicked me or something, you wouldn't do anything about it. I would overreact and start going off to them and trying to kick them.

TUTOR: You need to calm down right now.

STUDENT: I know that the teachers think that I'm bad, so they'll just go for their side. I don't bother to say anything.

task.content: Reporting a peer conflict or bullying incident to a teacher

context.environment: Mainstream school setting or residential school office
context.social_setting: One-on-one interaction with a teacher or staff member after a peer conflict

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the student attempts to report being called names or hurt, and the teacher dismisses the concern ('Oh yeah, we'll touch on this later') or instantly takes the other kids' side
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Stops bothering to say anything to teachers, withdraws from reporting, or overreacts and starts going off to the teachers
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Believes teachers think they are bad, feels teachers don't actually care and will forget, feels unfairly treated
related_quotes: I know that the teachers think that I ’m bad, so they ’ll just go for their side, so I don ’t bother to say anything.
If someone hurt me, like kicked me or something, they (the teachers) wouldn ’t do anything about it. I would overreact and start going off to them

behavior_patterns.condition: When teachers fail to address the initial provocation from peers and instead use physical restraint on the student
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Overreacts, tries to kick the peers who initiated the hurt, struggles against restraint
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Feels victimized by both peers and staff, perceives intervention as unfair
related_quotes: I would overreact and start going off to them and trying to kick them. So they (the teachers) forced me to the ground, not the kids that kicked me.
